have h₁ : (∫ x in 0..π, sin x * f (cos x)) = ∫ y in -1..1, f y := by
    have h₂ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) = ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) := rfl
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the change of variables theorem with y = cos x
    have h₃ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) = ∫ y in (cos π)..(cos 0), f y := by
      -- Apply the change of variables theorem
      have h₄ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) = ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) := rfl
      rw [h₄]
      have h₅ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) = ∫ y in (cos π)..(cos 0), f y := by
        -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
        have h₆ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, sin x * f (cos x) = ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, (deriv (fun x : ℝ => cos x) x) * f (cos x) := by
          congr
          ext x
          -- Show that the derivative of cos x is -sin x
          have h₇ : deriv (fun x : ℝ => cos x) x = -sin x := by
            simp [Real.differentiableAt_cos, deriv_cos]
          rw [h₇]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> simp [sin_neg]
          <;> ring_nf
        rw [h₆]
        -- Apply the substitution rule
        have h₈ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, (deriv (fun x : ℝ => cos x) x) * f (cos x) = ∫ y in (cos π)..(cos 0), f y := by
          -- Use the substitution rule for integrals

            -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
            have h₁₀ : ∫ x in (0 : ℝ)..π, (deriv (fun x : ℝ => cos x) x) * f (cos x) = ∫ y in (cos π)..(cos 0), f y := by
              -- Use the substitution rule for integrals
              rw [intervalIntegral.integral_comp_sub_left (fun x => f x)]
              <;> simp [cos_zero, cos_pi]
              <;> ring_nf
              <;> norm_num
              <;>
                (try

              <;>
                (try

                    <;> ring_nf
                    <;> norm_num

              <;>
                (try

                    linarith [Real.pi_pos]

            rw [h₁₀]
          rw [h₉]
        rw [h₈]

    rw [h₃]
    -- Simplify the limits of integration
